title: Systems and method for removal of acid gas in a circulating dry scrubber
abstract: Systems and methods for the use of highly reactive hydrated lime (HRH) in circulating dry scrubbers (CDS) to remove sulfur dioxide (SO2) from the flue gas.
claims: Claims 1. A system for removal of sulfur dioxide (SO2) from a flue gas, the system comprising: a flue gas duct including a circulating dry scrubber (CDS); and an injection system for injecting a particulate lime hydrate wherein: 90% percent of the particles in said particulate lime hydrate are less than or equal to about 10 microns and greater than or equal to about 4 microns in size; a ratio of a size of particle said 90% of said particles are below to a size of particle 10% of said particles are below is less than 8; and said particles have a BET surface area of about 18 m 2 /g or greater. 2. The system of claim 1 wherein the ratio is less than about 6. 3. The system of claim 1 wherein the ratio is between about 4 and about 7. 4. The system of claim I wherein the ratio is between about 5 and about 6. 5. The system of claim I wherein 90% percent of the particles are less than or equal to about 8 microns and greater than or equal to about 4 microns. 6. The system of claim I wherein 90% percent of the particles are less than or equal to about 6 microns and greater than or equal to about 4 microns. 7. The system of claim 1 wherein 90% percent of the particles are less man or equal to about 5 microns and greater than or equal to about 4 microns. 8. The system of claim I wherein the particles have a BET surface area of about 20 m 2 /g or greater. 9. The system of claim 1 wherein 50% of the particles arc less than or equal to about 4 microns. 10. The system of claim 1 wherein 50% of the particles are less than or equal to about 2 microns. 11. A method for removal of sulfur dioxide (SO2) from a flue gas, the method comprising: providing a flue gas duct including a circulating dry scrubber (CDS); and injecting a particulate lime hydrate having into said CDS; wherein: 90% percent of the particles in said particulate lime hydrate are less than or equal to about 10 microns and greater than or equal to about 4 microns in size; a ratio of a size of particle said 90% of said particles are below to a size of particle 10% of said particles are below is less than 8; and said particles have a BET surface area of about 18 m 2 /g or greater. 12. The system of claim 1 wherein the ratio is less than about 6. 13. The system of claim 1 wherein the ratio is between about 4 and about 7. 14. The system of claim 1 wherein the ratio is between about 5 and about 6. 15. The system of claim 1 wherein 90% percent of the particles are less man or equal to about 8 microns and greater than or equal to about 4 microns. 16. The system of claim 1 wherein 90% percent of the particles are less than or equal to about 6 microns and greater than or equal to about 4 microns. 17. The system of claim 1 wherein 90% percent of the particles are less than or equal to about S microns and greater than or equal to about 4 microns. 18. The system of claim 1 wherein the particles have a BET surface area of about 20 nr/g or greater. 19. The system of claim 1 wherein 50% of the particles are less than or equal to about 4 microns. 20. The system of claim I wherein 50% of the particles are less than or equal to about 2 microns. 21. A system for removal of sulfur dioxide (S02) from a flue gas, the system comprising: a flue gas duct including a circulating dry scrubber (CDS); and an injection system for injecting a highly reactive lime hydrate (HRH) into said flue gas in said CDS. 22. A method for removal of sulfur dioxide (S02) from a flue gas, the method comprising: providing a flue gas duct including a circulating dry scrubber (CDS); and injecting a highly reactive lime hydrate (HRH) into said flue gas in said CDS.
